steps.title: Identify the claim
steps.content: The historian claims that carpa theater's "audience-mediated slapstick comedy" influenced El Teatro's early performances. The right quote must show BOTH (1) physical/slapstick humor AND (2) the audience shaping the performance.

steps.title: Find the quote that hits both elements
steps.content: Choice A describes "exaggerated physical humor" (slapstick) performed for workers whose "reactions—enthusiastic cheers... and... loud boos—promoted improvisation" (audience-mediated). Both halves of the claim are illustrated.

steps.title: Eliminate the others
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'exaggerated physical humor... reactions... promoted improvisation': hits both slapstick and audience mediation. ✓
  • Choice B — 'humor was often found in that reality... social critique': mentions comedy but says nothing about slapstick or audience interaction. ✗
  • Choice C — 'satire, humor... rasquache... sociopolitical message': covers aesthetic and politics, not physical comedy or audience mediation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'nonprofessional actors... minimal props... brief humorous vignettes': describes who performed and where, but not slapstick style or audience shaping the show. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
